clusters.title: King Charles visit to US to proceed after Washington shooting

versions.title: King Charles visit to US to proceed after Washington shooting

clusters.title: Sri Lanka’s missing Balance Sheet

versions.title: Sri Lanka’s missing Balance Sheet
versions.deck: Sri Lanka’s crisis is often described in familiar terms: excessive borrowing, weak fiscal discipline and unsustainable debt. This diagnosis is not wrong. But it is incomplete. At its core, the crisis reflects a deeper issue in how sovereign financial positions are assessed and managed. Fiscal analysis remains overwhelmingly focused on flows – deficits, debt servicing and financing needs – while the stock of assets underpinning the public sector

clusters.title: Sri Lanka welcomes over 100,000 tourists in first 23 days of April

versions.title: Sri Lanka welcomes over 100,000 tourists in first 23 days of April
versions.deck: Sri Lanka’s tourism sector showed clear signs of cooling in April, even as the country edged close to the 100,000 mark in arrivals during the first three weeks of the month. According to the Sri Lanka Tourism Development Authority (SLTDA), the country welcomed 99,777 visitors between 1 and 22 April, marking a 26% decline year-on-year (YoY) compared to 133,979 arrivals in the same period last year. versions.title: Energy supplies are crashing: What must Sri Lanka do?
versions.deck: According to Kpler, a global business intelligence company, seaborne crude and condensate exports were down 6.9 million bpd in March compared to the previous month, and are down another 1.8 million bpd through 20 April. This leads to the conclusion that loadings are likely to be down 8.8 million bpd from prewar levels. clusters.title: Choosing disaster over prevention and mitigation: Wilful blindness or short-sighted idiocy?

versions.title: Choosing disaster over prevention and mitigation: Wilful blindness or short-sighted idiocy?
versions.deck: Sri Lanka has been a serial victim of a brutal cocktail of natural and man-made macro disasters in recent times. These twin forces have not only claimed thousands of lives but have systematically crippled the country’s economic foundation, exposing a dangerous void in proactive national planning. This relentless series of disasters has ranged from devastating natural phenomena to man-made catastrophes. The 2004 Indian Ocean Tsunami, triggered by

versions.title: Primary surplus almost doubles as Budget deficit nearly eliminated by end-Jan. 2026
versions.deck: Sri Lanka’s fiscal position strengthened markedly in January 2026, with a sharp expansion in the primary surplus and a near-elimination of the overall Budget deficit, according to data released by the Central Bank of Sri Lanka (CBSL) last Friday. versions.title: Endeavour of humanising technology: Enriching encounters
versions.deck: Artificial Intelligence (AI) is all around us. Technology is making massive advances for both construction as well as destruction. In such a context, I enjoyed reading an “old but gold” book by Erich Fromm with an apt title “The Revolution of Hope.” The tag line shows us the essence of his endeavour “towards a humanised technology.” Today’s column reflects on its resounding insights relevant to a world ravaged by technology warfare with wide imp

clusters.title: Credit card base, balances see marginal growth YTD end-Feb.

versions.title: Credit card base, balances see marginal growth YTD end-Feb.
versions.deck: Sri Lanka’s credit card base expanded in February, with the total number of active cards rising 1.3% compared to December 2025 to 2,193,380 from 2,166,186, according to latest data. The increase was driven by globally accepted cards, which grew 1.3% to 2,184,600 from 2,157,271. Locally accepted cards declined 1.5% to 8,780 from 8,915.

clusters.title: Beyond automation: Human skills powering finance in the AI era

versions.title: Beyond automation: Human skills powering finance in the AI era
versions.deck: Artificial intelligence (AI) is reshaping economies, industries, and professions at an extraordinary pace. For finance professionals, this transformation is both a challenge and an opportunity. The question is no longer whether AI will affect finance, but how quickly professionals can adapt to remain relevant and valuable. Sri Lanka’s ambitions underscore the urgency.
